data.title: Elica: Strategic Transformation Supports Growth but Margin Pressure and Weak Demand Justify Hold at EUR 1.70 Target
data.description:
Intesa Sanpaolo analyst Alberto Francese maintained a Hold rating on Elica S.p.A. yesterday and set a price target of €1.70.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alberto Francese has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Elica’s current positioning and outlook. The company is successfully transforming into a broader cooking player, with direct distribution in North America and growth in both cooking and motor divisions supporting revenues despite weak end markets.

However, this strategic shift is weighing on profitability, as higher promotional activity, an adverse price-mix, and transformation costs have compressed margins. While forecasts indicate gradual margin recovery and a solid balance sheet, persistent demand softness, sector overcapacity, and uncertainties in the U.S. DIY OEM channel limit near‑term upside, justifying a Neutral stance at the new EUR 1.70 target price.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Structural Shift in German Heating Market Drives LU-VE Upside: Initiating Buy with €50 Target
data.description:
TP ICAP MIDCAP analyst Michele Mombelli maintained a Buy rating on LU-VE SpA on February 16 and set a price target of €50.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Michele Mombelli has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to the structural shift in the German heating market. Heat pumps have rapidly become Germany’s leading heating technology, while sales of traditional oil and gas boilers have slumped, creating a powerful tailwind for suppliers of heat‑pump components such as LU‑VE.

Moreover, the EU‑approved €3bn German plan to boost cleantech and heat‑pump production provides long‑term policy support and visibility for OEM investment, which should benefit LU‑VE’s exchanger business. With heat‑pump revenues still a modest share of sales but growing at sustained double‑digit rates through 2026, Mombelli sees attractive upside potential, justifying a Buy rating and a €50 target price.

According to TipRanks, Mombelli is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 6.2% and a 53.33% success rate.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
